macro_summary: Peak Mag-7 earnings week collides with a FOMC rate decision (July 28-29), creating elevated implied volatility across tech and semis. TSLA and GOOGL report tonight, followed by MSFT on July 29 and AAPL/NVDA in subsequent weeks. The AI infrastructure super-cycle remains the dominant macro theme, with hyperscaler capex projected above $600B for 2026 and memory/chip shortages extending into 2027+. A ~25% market-implied probability of a 25bps Fed hike is the key tail risk that could pressure the entire tech complex.
screening_note: 6 independent screening runs were analyzed over the past 24 hours. The minimum appearance threshold was set at 2/6 runs. All 5 qualifying ideas cleared this bar, with 2 achieving perfect 6/6 consensus.
key_events: TSLA + GOOGL earnings tonight July 22 after close — QQQ/semis volatility trigger
SKHY (SK Hynix ADR) Q2 2026 earnings tonight July 22 after close; conference call July 29
FOMC rate decision July 29 ~2:00 PM ET — ~25% implied probability of 25bps hike
MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29 after close; Morgan Stanley $600 Overweight initiated July 21
AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30 after close; CXMT chip supply regulatory decision concurrent binary risk
NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26 after close; MSFT capex guidance July 29 is key read-through

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 6 overnight screens — the strongest consensus of the session. The world's #1 HBM memory supplier just listed on Nasdaq (July 10) and reports Q2 2026 earnings tonight after close, with management on record stating AI-driven memory demand exceeds supply through 2030+. The stock has pulled back ~22% from its $194.80 post-debut high to near its $149 IPO support level, while Barclays has initiated Overweight citing ~70% upside and revenue is already tracking +40.8% YoY and +58.3% QoQ.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings report tonight July 22 after close; analyst conference call July 29 (Seoul time); Barclays Overweight initiation already on record
ideas.edge: Trading ~$151 vs. avg 12-month analyst consensus target of $281.67 (~86% implied upside); entire 2026 HBM/DRAM/NAND output reportedly sold out; server DRAM prices expected +18% in Q3 2026; IPO-price technical base at $149 provides a defined stop level
ideas.bull_case: A capacity-sold-out Q2 beat tonight with raised guidance could re-rate the ADR toward the $194.80 post-IPO high and beyond; structural HBM shortage and Barclays' 70% upside target give multiple catalysts for revaluation over the next 3-6 months.
ideas.bear_case: Stock is a very recent Nasdaq listing with thin options liquidity and a history of ±10% daily swings since debut; any macro shock (FOMC hike surprise July 29) or miss on Q2 margins could flush the ADR back below $149 IPO support with limited market-maker support.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in all 6 overnight screens with the highest conviction vote count (4 high). The company reports Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30 after close off the back of a strong iPhone supercycle (+22% iPhone sales YoY) and record Services revenue (~$31B/qtr), but the stock trades at a rich 37x P/E leaving no margin for error. A concurrent CXMT chip-supply regulatory decision introduces a binary geopolitical risk, making defined-risk options structures (call spreads or straddles) strongly preferred over outright stock at these levels.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30 after close; concurrent CXMT chip-supply regulatory decision; rumored fall foldable iPhone debut as medium-term catalyst
ideas.edge: Options implied move ~5%+ around the print; stock at ~$284 vs. consensus target ~$315 (~11% gap) with $100B buyback providing a floor; 43-analyst Buy consensus; RSI flagged at 82 (overbought) creating a symmetric risk setup — $335 upside vs. $318 support on a miss
ideas.bull_case: FY2026 guidance of 14-17% revenue growth (Q2 already delivered 17% at $111.2B) plus Services flywheel and buyback support could push the stock through $315 consensus and toward $335 on a clean beat-and-raise.
ideas.bear_case: RSI at 82 signals overbought conditions; an adverse CXMT ruling or any tariff escalation could compound a valuation de-rate at 37x P/E; a 5% miss scenario points to $318 support with further downside if macro deteriorates into the FOMC.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in 5 of 6 runs with a perfect 5-for-5 high-conviction vote — the most consistent conviction signal in the digest. The company reports Q4 FY2026 on July 29 at its cheapest forward multiple since 2023 (21-22x NTM), with a fresh Morgan Stanley Overweight at $600 initiated July 21. The key swing variables are Azure cloud growth and FY2027 capex guidance, which also serve as the primary AI-infrastructure read-through for NVDA and the broader semis complex. Critically, July 29 overlap with the FOMC decision makes defined-risk call spreads (Aug expiry) structurally superior to outright stock.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29 after close — same day as FOMC rate decision; Morgan Stanley $600 Overweight initiated July 21; Azure growth and FY2027 capex guidance are the swing variables
ideas.edge: Trading at ~21-22x NTM forward P/E — cheapest since 2023 — vs. consensus analyst target ~$590-$592 (97 analysts, mostly Strong Buy) implying 20%+ upside; fresh $600 Morgan Stanley target; Q3 2026 revenue guidance set at $80.7B-$81.8B with full-year EPS consensus $16.46-$17.10
ideas.bull_case: A strong Azure growth beat and raised FY2027 AI capex commitment would simultaneously validate MSFT's Copilot monetization thesis and act as a bullish read-through for the entire AI infrastructure chain, likely propelling the stock toward the $590-$600 target range.
ideas.bear_case: Azure growth decelerating or cautious FY2027 guidance — combined with a surprise 25bps Fed hike the same afternoon — could pressure the multiple sharply; at 21-22x, the stock would re-rate quickly on any negative capex signal given its role as an AI bellwether.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 4 of 6 runs as the anchoring AI-infrastructure trade. The stock trades at a near-decade-low forward P/E of ~22x despite 212% EPS growth over 9 quarters and consensus EPS upward revisions of +6.69% in the past 3 months, creating a compelling valuation gap. The hard catalyst is August 26 earnings ($93.5B revenue consensus), but this week's MSFT (July 29) and GOOGL capex commentaries serve as an important near-term read-through chain. Sep-expiry call options are the preferred structure to bridge both the FOMC event and the August print.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26 after close (consensus EPS $2.12, revenue $93.5B); MSFT July 29 AI capex guidance as key near-term read-through; global semis rally continuation (KOSPI AI bellwether +4.6% overnight)
ideas.edge: Stock ~$206 vs. consensus analyst PT of $302.83 (~47% implied upside gap); forward P/E ~22x near decade low despite dominant AI GPU position; 61/61 analyst Strong Buy consensus; hyperscaler AI capex projected $600B+ in 2026; H1 2026 underperformed AMD (+70%) and MU (+45%) — mean-reversion potential
ideas.bull_case: Hyperscaler capex confirmation from MSFT/GOOGL earnings this week could spark a catch-up rally from NVDA's H1 underperformance, with the August 26 earnings print at $93.5B revenue consensus providing a second catalyst; 22x forward P/E leaves substantial re-rating room.
ideas.bear_case: A hawkish FOMC surprise on July 29 or disappointing capex commentary from MSFT could reprice AI infrastructure multiples broadly; NVDA has already lagged peers significantly in H1 2026, and thin options liquidity in longer-dated strikes can create unfavorable bid-ask spreads on Sep calls.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 4 of 6 runs exclusively as a hedge and event-volatility structure rather than a directional long. The simultaneous cluster of peak Mag-7 earnings (TSLA/GOOGL tonight, MSFT July 29, AAPL July 30) overlapping exactly with the FOMC rate decision on July 29 creates an unusually dense binary-event calendar. Screens consistently flagged QQQ as the cleanest vehicle to either hedge existing tech-long books via put spreads or to exploit elevated implied vol via calendar/condor structures, rather than expressing single-stock directional risk.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC rate decision July 29 2:00 PM ET (~25% implied 25bps hike probability); TSLA + GOOGL earnings tonight July 22; MSFT earnings July 29 (same session as FOMC); AAPL earnings July 30
ideas.edge: KOSPI-Nasdaq 100 correlation at 0.46 (2-year high vs. 5-year avg 0.16) signals global semis risk-on alignment; ~25% hike probability reprices sharply on a surprise; IV structurally elevated across the July 28-30 event window — favors premium structures or low-cost put hedges; QQQ provides diversified exposure without single-stock earnings binary concentration
ideas.bull_case: A FOMC hold (base case) combined with strong MSFT Azure and AAPL Services beats could trigger a synchronized Mag-7 rally, with semis correlation amplifying gains across the Nasdaq-100.
ideas.bear_case: A 25bps hike surprise on July 29 — landing simultaneously with MSFT earnings — could trigger a multi-sigma QQQ drawdown; at 3.50-3.75% Fed funds with core PCE at 3.3%, the macro backdrop does not fully rule out a hawkish shock.
